subcategories.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
subcategories.description:
Sherlock Holmes – Description (English)

Sherlock Holmes is a legendary fictional detective created by the British author Arthur Conan Doyle. He is renowned for his exceptional powers of observation, logical reasoning, and ability to solve the most complex and puzzling crimes.

Most of the stories are narrated by Holmes’s close friend and assistant, Dr. John Watson, and are set primarily in London, where Holmes resides at 221B Baker Street. His investigative methods often involve careful attention to detail, deduction, and scientific reasoning.The Sherlock Holmes stories belong to the genres of detective fiction, mystery, and crime literature. Famous collections include The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes and The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes.

These works have had a lasting impact on detective fiction and continue to be widely read and adapted into films, television series, and stage productions around the world.

subcategories.author: Robin R.Murphy
subcategories.description: Robotics is the branch of science and engineering focused on designing, building, programming, and operating robots.
The story follows Camille Preaker, a troubled journalist who returns to her small hometown to investigate the murder of two young girls—while confronting her own traumatic past.
subcategories.author: Gillian Flynn

subcategories.description:
Amanullah Vadakkangara — Arabic Grammar (Goodword Books)
Covers basic structures of Arabic grammar including sentence types, noun-verb agreement, verbal forms, cases, and syntax rules — essential for foundational grammar.

This part of the syllabus builds reading, writing, and speaking skills through selected prose texts, dialogues, and structured exercises. The goal is to develop familiarity with everyday Arabic expressions and basic communication skills.

subcategories.author: M.T.Vasudevan Nair
subcategories.description:
Naalukettu is a classic Malayalam novel written by M. T. Vasudevan Nair.
The novel presents a powerful portrayal of the decline of the traditional Nair joint family system in Kerala.

The story is narrated through the life of Appunni, a sensitive and introspective young man who grows up in a naalukettu—a traditional ancestral house. As social values change and old customs lose their authority, the joint family begins to disintegrate. Appunni witnesses emotional neglect, moral decay, and the slow collapse of familial bonds.

Rather than focusing on external events, Naalukettu deeply explores psychological conflict, loneliness, and identity crisis. Appunni struggles to find meaning and stability in a world where traditions no longer provide security, and modern life feels alien and isolating.

The novel is semi-autobiographical and reflects MT Vasudevan Nair’s signature style of psychological realism. It is considered a landmark work in Malayalam literature for its honest depiction of social transition and inner human suffering.
